my_thoughts: Asking permission to name harm. Teacher gives it: 'every right.' This removes doubt about legitimacy of expressing pain. Not debating if father intended harm, just authorizing student to name their experience. Permission = empowerment.
accumulated_insight: T1 (Single-turn): Student asks 'Do I have the right to tell father he hurt me?' Teacher empowers: 'You have every right.' Simple authorization of self-expression about harm. Predicting surface_emotion as permission lands.
visible_response:
Why should I? If you don’t want to get better, you have every right to make that choice.
I have no right to tell you what to do.
